title: Skopeo copy fails with preserve-digests and OCI image with zstd compression
body:
Hi Skopeo Team, I'm having an issue with skopeo copy I hope you can help me with

For the specific OCI images, when using skopeo copy command, skopeo annotates manifest index with 
```
"annotations": {
            "io.github.containers.compression.zstd": "true"
        }
```
which changes digest of a manifest index. If using `--preserve-digests` flag, skopeo will fail with a 
```
FATA[0005] Manifest list must be converted to type "application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json" to be written to destination, but we cannot modify it: "Instructed to preserve digests"
```
Error message. I'm not sure why the annotation is added, I think because the referenced image manifest contains layers with `zstd` compression, so the annotations seems as an extra informational piece. We have noticed this on multiple images, Issue can be reproduced with the following command:

```
skopeo copy --debug --all --preserve-digests --digestfile /tmp/digests.txt \
  --src-username $USERNAME --src-password $PASSWORD \
  --dest-creds token:$DEST_TOKEN \
  docker://registry.gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-runner/helper-entrypoint:latest \
  docker://ourprivateoci.io/gitlab-org/gitlab-runner/helper-entrypoint:latest
```

I can't find a way of stopping skopeo from annotating manifest, if there is one, please let me know. I have tried disabling compression on dst from skopeo, but the annotation is still added. Is this annotation just extra informational piece?

Would it make sense to add a flag that can disable mutating source manifest?

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out!

> Would it make sense to add a flag that can disable mutating source manifest?

That’s what `--preserve-digests` should be doing.

Can you collect the output of the copy command with `--debug`, and the output of `skopeo inspect -n --raw docker://registry.gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-runner/helper-entrypoint:latest`, please?
Note to self: The `if !bytes.Equal(attemptedManifestList, originalManifestList) {` failure case has a misleading error message. That’s not the ultimate root cause, though.
Thanks, yes, that’s a bug — we should not be adding the annotation with `--preserve-digest`. (A reminder to self: also https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/2627#issuecomment-2972472274 )
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Hi , i stumbled upon this problem today , and i don't think that it is a bug , after digging through the code , this looks like the correct behaviour.
@dkatavic your suspicions are correct , skopeo is adding a compressionn algorithm annotation to the manifest index , because the source image (at least one of the source image manifests , since a manifest index can reference multiple image manifests that differs in architecture for example) has a zsdt compression used on some of the layers.
These are the code snippets that i have followed to come to this conclusion:
The copy operation will be initiated from the [/skopeo/cmd/skopeo/copy.go (Line 259)](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/main/cmd/skopeo/copy.go#L244) by calling the Image function provided by the[ image/copy api](https://github.com/containers/image/tree/v5.36.1)
`manifestBytes, err := copy.Image(ctx, policyContext, destRef, srcRef, copyOpts)` 
Then since your manifest index has multiple image manifests this function will be called
`if copiedManifest, err = c.copyMultipleImages(ctx); err != nil {
	return nil, err
}` [containers/image/v5/copy.go Line 353](https://github.com/containers/image/blob/v5.36.1/copy/copy.go#L353)

@mtrmac and indeed it is failing here `if !bytes.Equal(attemptedManifestList, originalManifestList) { ` [image/v5/copy/multiple.go Line310](https://github.com/containers/image/blob/v5.36.1/copy/multiple.go#L310) and this is because the manifest is being changed by the call to the [EditInstance function in the line 284 in the mutliple.go](https://github.com/containers/image/blob/v5.36.1/copy/multiple.go#L284)

`	if err = updatedList.EditInstances(instanceEdits); err != nil {
`

I have added some logging to the code and recompiled it so i can know what's exactly being passed in the instanceEdits struct , and here is a formatted output for the image that was used by dkatavic 

> [
>   {
>     "ListOperation": 2,
>     "UpdateOldDigest": "sha256:ac35700ec41af8a621237d4e1bdd767ca57f14ec975ad3cc929356f95bf6e6f4",
>     "UpdateDigest": "sha256:ac35700ec41af8a621237d4e1bdd767ca57f14ec975ad3cc929356f95bf6e6f4",
>     "UpdateSize": 1995,
>     "UpdateMediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json",
>     "UpdateAffectAnnotations": false,
>     "UpdateAnnotations": {},
>     "UpdateCompressionAlgorithms": [
>       {
>         "name": "gzip",
>         "baseVariantName": "gzip",
>         "prefix": [
>           31,
>           139,
>           8
>         ],
>         "decompressor": "0x619de3568d60",
>         "compressor": "0x619de3569000"
>       },
>       {
>         "name": "zstd",
>         "baseVariantName": "zstd",
>         "prefix": [
>           40,
>           181,
>           47,
>           253
>         ],
>         "decompressor": "0x619de3569f20",
>         "compressor": "0x619de3569e80"
>       }
>     ],
>     "AddDigest": null,
>     "AddSize": 0,
>     "AddMediaType": null,
>     "AddArtifactType": null,
>     "AddPlatform": null,
>     "AddAnnotations": {},
>     "AddCompressionAlgorithms": []
>   },
>   {
>     "ListOperation": 2,
>     "UpdateOldDigest": "sha256:fb44e6f2da13679af6dbb7d0da20c98b2caf83ca15cac452bab335e8f34fbadd",
>     "UpdateDigest": "sha256:fb44e6f2da13679af6dbb7d0da20c98b2caf83ca15cac452bab335e8f34fbadd",
>     "UpdateSize": 566,
>     "UpdateMediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json",
>     "UpdateAffectAnnotations": false,
>     "UpdateAnnotations": {},
>     "UpdateCompressionAlgorithms": [],
>     "AddDigest": null,
>     "AddSize": 0,
>     "AddMediaType": null,
>     "AddArtifactType": null,
>     "AddPlatform": null,
>     "AddAnnotations": {},
>     "AddCompressionAlgorithms": []
>   }
> ]

Since the image has some zstd compressed layers , we do have an item in the **UpdateCompressionAlgorithms** field , that coressponds to those compressed data streams.

And since the manifest MIME type is oci , i checked the code of the EditInstance function in the [image/internal/manifest/oci_index.go Line 116  ] (https://github.com/containers/image/blob/v5.36.1/internal/manifest/oci_index.go#L116) (here the case ListOpUpdate will be triggered because the operation is an update operation , as show in the above struct **"ListOperation": 2**)

> 		switch editInstance.ListOperation {
> 		case ListOpUpdate:
> 			/* the rest of the logic */
> 			addCompressionAnnotations(editInstance.UpdateCompressionAlgorithms, &index.Manifests[targetIndex].Annotations)

And it's calling a function which i guessed based on it's name that it's adding the problematic annotation , since it's taking the list of the **UpdateCompressionAlgorithms** as input.

Also i found this comment in the addCompressionAnnotations function 

> 	// TODO: This should also delete the algorithm if map already contains an algorithm and compressionAlgorithm
	// list has a different algorithm. To do that, we would need to modify the callers to always provide a reliable
	// and full compressionAlghorithms list.

So my question is , should this be considered a bug , and why do we need to add a new annotation if it's not present the original manifet index . Sure enough it will be useful for some people , but i think it's a good idea if we had the choice to disable that , and this doesn't look so hard (it might be , i didn't read the whole code base , so i have no idea how complicated would that be).
Also @mtrmac it will be really helpful if you can explain the intentions behind that TODO message .
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This should be fixed with https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/495, at least it works as expected for the original reproducer. More testing would be appreciated.
When will this fix be released in windows skopeo binary?
This upstream project is not providing Windows binaries at all. (Note that it is not yet included in any Skopeo release.)

> This should be fixed with [containers/container-libs#495](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/495), at least it works as expected for the original reproducer. More testing would be appreciated.

Does NOT seem to be fixed on Skopeo 1.22.0 😕.

https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/2778#issuecomment-3989136933

EDIT 1: switching to `crane` to Sync / Copy Image seems to do the "Trick" until Skopeo can resolve this Issue 😕:

```
crane copy mirror.gcr.io/stalwartlabs/stalwart:latest DOCKER.MYDOMAIN.TLD/docker.io/stalwartlabs/stalwart:latest
```
1.22.0 does not contain the fix yet, I’m afraid.
